content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to conduct a lecture for which he needs to set up an online meeting of exactly XXX minutes.

The meeting platform supports a meeting of maximum 303030 minutes without subscription and a meeting of unlimited duration with subscription.

Determine whether Chef needs to take a subscription or not for setting up the meet.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single integer XXX - denoting the duration of the lecture.


Output Format
For each test case, print in a single line, `YES` if Chef needs to take the subscription, otherwise print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the required meeting duration in minutes.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a line containing "YES" if Chef needs a subscription (i.e., X > 30), otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: For X=50 (>30) subscription needed → YES. X=3 (≤30) → NO. X=30 (≤30) → NO. X=80 (>30) → YES.
1 and 30 are within the free limit, so NO. 31 exceeds the limit, so YES.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read X and compare it with the free limit 30. If X is greater than 30, print YES, otherwise print NO. This is O(1) work per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
